 807 |     /// Convert our generic messages to Bedrock Converse API format.
 808 |     ///
 809 |     /// Bedrock Converse uses:
 810 |     /// - system prompt as a top-level "system" array
 811 |     /// - messages with "role" and "content" array
 812 |     /// - tool_use blocks in assistant content
 813 |     /// - toolResult blocks in user content
 814 |     ///
 815 |     /// IMPORTANT: Bedrock requires strict role alternation (user/assistant).
 816 |     /// Consecutive Role::Tool messages must be merged into a single "user"
 817 |     /// message so all toolResult blocks for a given assistant turn appear
 818 |     /// together. Consecutive same-role messages are also merged to prevent
 819 |     /// validation errors.
 820 |     fn convert_messages(messages: &[Message]) -> (Vec<Value>, Vec<Value>) {
 821 |         let mut system_parts: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();
 822 |         let mut api_messages: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();
 823 | 
 824 |         for msg in messages {
 825 |             match msg.role {
 826 |                 Role::System => {
 827 |                     let text: String = msg
 828 |                         .content
 829 |                         .iter()
 830 |                         .filter_map(|p| match p {
 831 |                             ContentPart::Text { text } => Some(text.clone()),
 832 |                             _ => None,
 833 |                         })
 834 |                         .collect::<Vec<_>>()
 835 |                         .join("\n");
 836 |                     if !text.trim().is_empty() {
 837 |                         system_parts.push(json!({"text": text}));
 838 |                     }
 839 |                 }
 840 |                 Role::User => {
 841 |                     let mut content_parts: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();
 842 |                     for part in &msg.content {
 843 |                         if let ContentPart::Text { text } = part
 844 |                             && !text.trim().is_empty()
 845 |                         {
 846 |                             content_parts.push(json!({"text": text}));
 847 |                         }
 848 |                     }
 849 |                     if !content_parts.is_empty() {
 850 |                         // Merge into previous user message if the last message is also "user"
 851 |                         if let Some(last) = api_messages.last_mut()
 852 |                             && last.get("role").and_then(|r| r.as_str()) == Some("user")
 853 |                             && let Some(arr) =
 854 |                                 last.get_mut("content").and_then(|c| c.as_array_mut())
 855 |                         {
 856 |                             arr.extend(content_parts);
 857 |                             continue;
 858 |                         }
 859 |                         api_messages.push(json!({
 860 |                             "role": "user",
 861 |                             "content": content_parts
 862 |                         }));
 863 |                     }
 864 |                 }
 865 |                 Role::Assistant => {
 866 |                     let mut content_parts: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();
 867 |                     for part in &msg.content {
 868 |                         match part {
 869 |                             ContentPart::Text { text } => {
 870 |                                 if !text.trim().is_empty() {
 871 |                                     content_parts.push(json!({"text": text}));
 872 |                                 }
 873 |                             }
 874 |                             ContentPart::ToolCall {
 875 |                                 id,
 876 |                                 name,
 877 |                                 arguments,
 878 |                                 ..
 879 |                             } => {
 880 |                                 let input: Value = serde_json::from_str(arguments)
 881 |                                     .unwrap_or_else(|_| json!({"raw": arguments}));
 882 |                                 content_parts.push(json!({
 883 |                                     "toolUse": {
 884 |                                         "toolUseId": id,
 885 |                                         "name": name,
 886 |                                         "input": input
 887 |                                     }
 888 |                                 }));
 889 |                             }
 890 |                             _ => {}
 891 |                         }
 892 |                     }
 893 |                     // Bedrock rejects whitespace-only text blocks; if the assistant message has
 894 |                     // no usable content (e.g. thinking-only), drop it from the request.
 895 |                     if content_parts.is_empty() {
 896 |                         continue;
 897 |                     }
 898 |                     // Merge into previous assistant message if consecutive
 899 |                     if let Some(last) = api_messages.last_mut()
 900 |                         && last.get("role").and_then(|r| r.as_str()) == Some("assistant")
 901 |                         && let Some(arr) = last.get_mut("content").and_then(|c| c.as_array_mut())
 902 |                     {
 903 |                         arr.extend(content_parts);
 904 |                         continue;
 905 |                     }
 906 |                     api_messages.push(json!({
 907 |                         "role": "assistant",
 908 |                         "content": content_parts
 909 |                     }));
 910 |                 }
 911 |                 Role::Tool => {
 912 |                     // Tool results must be in a "user" message with toolResult blocks.
 913 |                     // Merge into the previous user message if one exists (handles
 914 |                     // consecutive Tool messages being collapsed into one user turn).
 915 |                     let mut content_parts: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();
 916 |                     for part in &msg.content {
 917 |                         if let ContentPart::ToolResult {
 918 |                             tool_call_id,
 919 |                             content,
 920 |                         } = part
 921 |                         {
 922 |                             let content = if content.trim().is_empty() {
 923 |                                 "(empty tool result)".to_string()
 924 |                             } else {
 925 |                                 content.clone()
 926 |                             };
 927 |                             content_parts.push(json!({
 928 |                                 "toolResult": {
 929 |                                     "toolUseId": tool_call_id,
 930 |                                     "content": [{"text": content}],
 931 |                                     "status": "success"
 932 |                                 }
 933 |                             }));
 934 |                         }
 935 |                     }
 936 |                     if !content_parts.is_empty() {
 937 |                         // Merge into previous user message (from earlier Tool messages)
 938 |                         if let Some(last) = api_messages.last_mut()
 939 |                             && last.get("role").and_then(|r| r.as_str()) == Some("user")
 940 |                             && let Some(arr) =
 941 |                                 last.get_mut("content").and_then(|c| c.as_array_mut())
 942 |                         {
 943 |                             arr.extend(content_parts);
 944 |                             continue;
 945 |                         }
 946 |                         api_messages.push(json!({
 947 |                             "role": "user",
 948 |                             "content": content_parts
 949 |                         }));
 950 |                     }
 951 |                 }
 952 |             }
 953 |         }
 954 | 
 955 |         (system_parts, api_messages)

```
  left: "claude-opus-4-5"
 right: "us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0"

```

But the input "claude-opus-4.5" should match... unless the input has a hyphen instead of a period.

Looking at the test: `BedrockProvider::resolve_model_id("claude-opus-4-5")` - it's using a hyphen! But the match expects a period: `"claude-opus-4.5"`.

```
"claude-opus-4-5"

```
"claude-opus-4.5"
